history.content:
<thought>
I see that I am facing forward at row 7 column D with a wall (#) directly in front of me, so I cannot move forward. To progress and explore new areas that might lead to the goal, I will turn right to face east where there seems to be open floor cells ('.'). This will allow me to move forward safely in the next steps.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing forward at cell D7, which is a floor cell. My path forward is blocked by a wall at E7. To find a way to the goal, I should explore left or right. Looking at the map, the left side has many lava cells, while the right side is blocked by a wall at E7. Moving forward is possible, so I will move forward into the safe cell D6.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am currently facing forward at position D07. The cell directly in front of me at D06 is '.', which is a safe floor cell. Since the goal is not yet visible, moving forward into a safe cell is the best next step to explore further.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at row 7, column D, facing forward. The cell directly in front of me (row 6, column D) is a floor cell (.) which is safe to move to. There is a wall (#) to the right (column E) and lava cells on the left in row 7 and above. Moving forward is the safest immediate action.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at row 07 column D facing north (upwards in the map). The cell directly ahead (row 06, column D) is an empty floor '.', which is safe to move onto. Since the goal is not currently visible, my best move is to advance forward to explore further and possibly find the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I see that I am facing forward at cell D07, which is a floor cell. The path forward goes to a wall at E07, so I cannot move that way. To the left at C07 is empty floor, and to the right at E07 is a wall. Since moving forward is blocked, I will turn left to face cell C07 which is safe and clear.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I see the goal at row 01 column F. The path directly ahead (row 06 column D) is clear (.), but the goal is far ahead. Moving forward seems safe and will bring me closer to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am currently at the bottom row, facing upward (since I am represented by ^). Directly ahead, the cells in column D are free (floor) or lava in some cells. I see the goal in row 2, column F, which is two cells ahead and two cells to the right. Moving forward is possible, but the path to the right of this column contains lava. To avoid lava and approach the goal, I should turn left to potentially find a safer route or move forward if clear.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at the bottom-center of the view at D07, facing forward. The cells directly in front of the agent (D06) is lava, which is deadly and must be avoided. To progress towards the goal at F03 while avoiding lava, I should explore another direction. The left cell (C07) is lava, the right cell (E07) is also lava. Moving forward is unsafe due to lava. Therefore, I will turn left to face toward C07 in case there is a possible way, and then decide further moves, since moving forward is unsafe.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at row 7, column D facing forward. The cell directly in front of me (row 6, column D) contains lava, so moving forward is unsafe. To find a safe path, I should turn to face a safe direction next. The cell to my left (row 7, column C) is free floor, so turning left will face that safe cell.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell in front of me is lava, which is deadly and must be avoided. To find a safe path, I should rotate to face a direction that has a safe floor cell. Turning right will face me toward column E at row 7 which appears to be a safe floor cell.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am currently at row 07, column D facing forward. The cell in front is a floor cell (row 06, column D) but it contains lava (L). Moving forward is dangerous. To reach the goal, I should avoid lava and walls. I will turn left to explore the left side, which looks safer based on visible floor cells.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing forward at row 07 column D. The cell directly in front is lava (L), which is deadly, so moving forward is unsafe. To find a safe path, I should turn left or right to explore other options. Looking at the cells around: turning left will orient me toward column C, which is lava, and turning right will orient me toward column E, which is also lava. However, turning left first allows me to potentially navigate around the lava safely as there are floor (.) cells nearby. I will turn left to continue exploring.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am currently facing forward where the cell is '.' (floor) at D7 in front of me. There is lava on the row above, but directly in front and to the side I see floor cells. It is safe to move forward and explore, especially since the goal could be ahead. I will move forward to D6.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
